articles.title: CVE-2026-63030 - WordPress Core Interpretation Conflict Vulnerability
articles.snippet: CVE: CVE-2026-63030 Description: WordPress Core contains an interpretation conflict vulnerability that could allow an attacker to perform SQL Injection and achieve Remote Code Execution. This vulnerability can be chained with CVE-2026-60137. Known Ransomware Usage: Unknown GitHub POCs: https://github.com/Ch4120N/CVE-2026-63030 https://github.com/lucifer0xf/wp2shell-Wordpress-TOWN https://github.co

articles.title: CVE-2026-60137 - WordPress Core SQL Injection Vulnerability
articles.snippet: CVE: CVE-2026-60137 Description: WordPress Core contains a SQL injection vulnerability when a plugin or theme passes untrusted input to the parameter. This vulnerability can be chained with CVE-2026-63030 to allow an unauthenticated attacker to gain remote code execution on default WordPress installations. Known Ransomware Usage: Unknown GitHub POCs: https://github.com/codeb0ssx/Ultimate-wp2shell

articles.title: CVE-2021-27137 - DD-WRT Stack-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability
articles.snippet: CVE: CVE-2021-27137 Description: DD-WRT contains a stack-based buffer overflow vulnerability that could allow an unauthenticated attacker to overflow an internal buffer used by UPnP and trigger a code execution vulnerability. This time, though, clients didn't have to take our word for it: 1.1.1.1 returned EDE 33, a new DNS error code that signals directly in the response that DNSSEC validation was bypassed.
